              generic "Skipped: Insufficient explicit relationship support: The technique describes rootkit evasion methods while the countermeasure describes vulnerability scanning processes. The relationship note only establishes a metadata linkage without substantive relational content." [e616] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "DE-0007->CM0011 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e617] [cursor=pointer]

            cell [e620] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0007 Technique Name: Evasion via Rootkit Technique Description: Rootkits are programs that hide the existence of malware by intercepting/hooking and modifying operating system API calls that supply system information. Rootkits or rootkit enabling functionality may reside at the flight software or kernel level in the operating system or lower, to include a hypervisor, Master Boot Record, or System Firmware. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0011 Countermeasure Name: Vulnerability Scanning Countermeasure Description: Vulnerability scanning is used to identify known software vulnerabilities (excluding custom-developed software - ex: COTS and Open-Source). Utilize scanning tools to identify vulnerabilities in dependencies and outdated software (i.e., software composition analysis). Ensure that vulnerability scanning tools and techniques are employed that facilitate interoperability among tools and automate parts of the vulnerability management process by using standards for: (1) Enumerating platforms, custom software flaws, and improper configurations; (2) Formatting checklists and test procedures; and (3) Measuring vulnerability impact. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0011 Vulnerability Scanning in technique DE-0007 Evasion via Rootkit countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." [e621] [cursor=pointer]
            cell "—" [e622] [cursor=pointer]
            cell "Skipped: Insufficient explicit relationship support: The technique describes rootkit evasion methods while the countermeasure describes vulnerability scanning processes. No direct connection between vulnerability scanning and rootkit detection/prevention is explicitly stated in the admissible source text. The relationship note only establishes a metadata linkage without substantive relational content." [e623] [cursor=pointer]

            cell "14855ms" [e625] [cursor=pointer]
            cell "deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1-TEE" [e626] [cursor=pointer]
          row [e627] [cursor=pointer]

There is no explicit connection between least privilege and whitelist modification in the admissible source text. The relationship note only establishes metadata linkage but provides no substantive connection between the concepts." [e629] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "DE-0006->CM0039 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e630] [cursor=pointer]

            cell [e633] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0006 Technique Name: Modify Whitelist Technique Description: Threat actors may target whitelists on the spacecrafts as a means to execute and/or hide malicious processes/programs. Whitelisting is a common technique used on traditional IT systems but has also been used on spacecrafts. Whitelisting is used to prevent execution of unknown or potentially malicious software. However, this technique can be bypassed if not implemented correctly but threat actors may also simply attempt to modify the whitelist outright to ensure their malicious software will operate on the spacecraft that utilizes whitelisting. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0039 Countermeasure Name: Least Privilege Countermeasure Description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized processes which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks in accordance with system functions. Ideally maintain a separate execution domain for each executing process. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0039 Least Privilege in technique DE-0006 Modify Whitelist countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." The relationship note only establishes metadata linkage but provides no substantive connection between the concepts." [e636] [cursor=pointer]

              generic "Complete: Countermeasure CM0002 COMSEC is relevant to technique DE-0005 because the technique involves threat actors exploiting safe mode to evade security controls and avoid detection, while COMSEC requires maintaining cryptographic security to prevent unauthorized disclosure and detect changes to information during transmission. Specifically, spacecraft should not employ a mode where cryptography can be disabled (crypto-bypass mode), which directly counters the safe mode exploitation described in the technique." [e642] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "DE-0005->CM0002 • create-qras-manifest-relationship-1776964279" [e643] [cursor=pointer]

            cell [e646] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Return exactly one top-level JSON object with keys: - pairs - skipped_reason Generate up to 3 grounded, non-duplicative QRA pairs about the relationship between this SPARTA technique and this SPARTA countermeasure. ## Technique Technique ID: DE-0005 Technique Name: Subvert Protections via Safe-Mode Technique Description: Threat actors may exploit safe mode to evade security controls and avoid detection by issuing commands or performing actions that would be blocked during nominal operations. In safe mode, spacecraft often disable telemetry filtering, authentication checks, or command restrictions to prioritize recovery, which can be subverted by an attacker to conceal malicious activity or establish a persistent foothold. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0002 Countermeasure Name: COMSEC Countermeasure Description: A component of cybersecurity to deny unauthorized persons information derived from telecommunications and to ensure the authenticity of such telecommunications. COMSEC includes cryptographic security, transmission security, emissions security, and physical security of COMSEC material. It is imperative to utilize secure communication protocols with strong cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of, and detect changes to, information during transmission. Systems should also maintain the confidentiality and integrity of information during preparation for transmission and during reception. Spacecraft should not employ a mode of operations where cryptography on the TT&C link can be disabled (i.e., crypto-bypass mode). The cryptographic mechanisms should identify and reject wireless transmissions that are deliberate attempts to achieve imitative or manipulative communications deception based on signal parameters. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0002 COMSEC in technique DE-0005 Subvert Protections via Safe-Mode countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." Specifically, spacecraft should not employ a mode where cryptography can be disabled (crypto-bypass mode), which directly counters the safe mode exploitation described in the technique." This can be done several ways, including through the manipulation of command headers, spoofing locations, or even leveraging Insider's access (i.e., Insider Threat) ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0035 Countermeasure Name: Protect Authenticators Countermeasure Description: Protect authenticator content from unauthorized disclosure and modification. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0035 Protect Authenticators in technique DE-0004 Masquerading countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs." In safe mode, spacecraft often disable telemetry filtering, authentication checks, or command restrictions to prioritize recovery, which can be subverted by an attacker to conceal malicious activity or establish a persistent foothold. ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0022 Countermeasure Name: Criticality Analysis Countermeasure Description: Conduct a criticality analysis to identify mission critical functions, critical components, and data flows and reduce the vulnerability of such functions and components through secure system design. Focus supply chain protection on the most critical components/functions. This can be done several ways, including through the manipulation of command headers, spoofing locations, or even leveraging Insider's access (i.e., Insider Threat) ## Countermeasure Countermeasure ID: CM0034 Countermeasure Name: Monitor Critical Telemetry Points Countermeasure Description: Monitor defined telemetry points for malicious activities (i.e., jamming attempts, commanding attempts (e.g., command modes, counters, etc.)). This would include valid/processed commands as well as commands that were rejected. Telemetry monitoring should synchronize with ground-based Defensive Cyber Operations (i.e., SIEM/auditing) to create a full space system situation awareness from a cybersecurity perspective. ## Optional Relationship Note Relationship Note: SPARTA metadata lists countermeasure CM0034 Monitor Critical Telemetry Points in technique DE-0004 Masquerading countermeasure_ids. ## Reminders - This prompt is about the relationship, not standalone node definitions. - Relationship Note, when present, is admissible structured SPARTA metadata establishing the direct linkage. - Do not infer relevance from IDs, naming, or hierarchy alone. - Every non-empty pair must have at least one quote from the technique and one quote from the countermeasure. - Relationship Note may establish the linkage but must not replace node evidence entirely. - If either side is non-substantive or placeholder-like, return zero pairs. - Preserve modality exactly. - Prefer fewer pairs over weak pairs. - If source support is insufficient, return zero pairs."
